name: Kasha Varnishkes
recipeIngredient: 1 Knorr bouillon cube dissolved in 2 cups boiling water
recipeInstructions:
1. Add 1½ teaspoons salt to the boiling chicken stock or water.
2. In a small bowl, mix the groats with egg until each groat is well coated with egg.
3. Place the mixture in a medium-sized saucepan over moderately high heat and stir constantly with a wooden spoon until the groats separate and the egg begins to dry.
4. Remove from heat and pour the boiling chicken stock or water over the kasha. Add salt and stir. Cover tightly and cook over low heat at a bare simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat and reserve.
5. Bring a quart of water to a boil and cook the pasta about 15 minutes, until tender. Drain and reserve.
6. Heat the oil in a skillet and sauté the onions until golden brown. Mix the onions, kasha, and cooked bowties together in a large bowl and serve. Serve with pot roast or just with leftover pot roast gravy.
Advice: For a more delicious and authentic version, substitute 1/4 cup of schmaltz for the corn oil.
